meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop chasing clients with an empty portfolio. Use this intermediate guide to build a high-conversion SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop waiting for clients and start building assets. By deploying a modular SaaS starter kit, you create a repeatable, high-value engine. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship professional-grade SaaS products in days, not months.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is the loudest barrier to your freelance career. Clients don't want to see 'potential'; they want to see the tangible architecture of a working, scalable SaaS product. When you lack a history of shipped work, you are effectively invisible in a saturated market.

Relief comes from shifting your strategy from 'applying for jobs' to 'shipping infrastructure.' A SaaS starter kit is your golden ticket—it is a production-ready boilerplate that proves your engineering prowess. With the right tooling, you aren't just writing code; you are building a proprietary foundation that turns every future client project into a high-margin, low-effort execution.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Core Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust database schema that handles multi-tenancy and authentication out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS starter kit including users, organizations, subscriptions, and role-based access control. Prioritize scalability and clean relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Prisma schema based on core SaaS entities'

steps.title: Scaffold the Authentication Layer
steps.description: Implement enterprise-grade auth. Never build this from scratch again; use established secure patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication flow using NextAuth or Clerk integration, focusing on secure session handling and email verification hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement secure authentication providers with session management'

steps.title: Build the Subscription Engine
steps.description: Connect your starter kit to Stripe to demonstrate your ability to handle recurring revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler for subscription status updates, including trial periods and failed payment logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate Stripe API with subscription billing logic'

steps.title: Develop the Component Library
steps.description: Build a modular UI system that makes your future client work look like a million-dollar app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a reusable component library using Tailwind CSS and Radix UI, focused on high-conversion SaaS landing pages.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Bootstrap Tailwind design system with accessible components'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Configure CI/CD to prove you understand professional DevOps workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel or Railway.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure CI/CD pipeline for automated production deployments'

pro_tips: Always build your starter kit as a Monorepo to allow for easy sharing of types between your frontend and backend.
Include a 'Demo Mode' toggle in your UI so prospective clients can instantly explore the dashboard without registration.
Document your architecture decisions in a README.md; clients pay more for developers who explain their 'why'.
Use mock data factories to ensure your UI looks 'lived-in' and professional the moment you present it.
Prioritize accessibility (a11y) early; it is a premium feature that sets your work apart from amateur freelancers.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live on GitHub, create a 60-second Loom video walking through the architecture. Send this link to three high-value prospects to demonstrate immediate value.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical edge to stop chasing and start building. Don't let your skills stay hidden behind a blank portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
